person_name: Jesus Christ (the Saviour/Redeemer in Christian theology)
person_name_full: Jesus Christ (the Saviour/Redeemer in Christian theology)
notes: Central divine figure of Christianity, regarded as the Saviour and Redeemer.

docs.title: Ecclesiastical History, Book 1
docs.author: Eusebius
docs.excerpt: … And there lived at that time Jesus , a wise man, if indeed it be proper to call him a man . For he was a doer of wonderful works , and a teacher of such men as receive the truth in gladness . And he attached to himself many of the Jews , and many also of the Greeks. He was the Christ . 8.

docs.title: Ecclesiastical History, Book 7
docs.author: Eusebius
docs.excerpt: And besides all this he rejects the holy baptism , and overturns the faith and confession which precede it, and entirely banishes from them the Holy Ghost , if indeed there was any hope that he would remain or

docs.title: Letter CVIII
docs.author: Jerome
docs.excerpt: Did not even the Saviour humble Himself, taking the form of a servant and being made obedient to the Father unto death, even the death of the cross, that He might save us by His passion?
